# moderation — ARCHITECTURE.md
# Last Updated: 2026-06-02
# Status: CURRENT SOURCE OF TRUTH — generated by ARCHITECT 2026-06-02

## Feature Overview

The moderation feature provides the full content and actor moderation surface for the VCSM platform. It handles user-initiated reporting of actors, posts, comments, messages, and conversations; actor-scoped content hiding (post/comment/conversation); and privileged moderator actions (global content hide, report dismissal). All moderation write operations that require elevated privileges are guarded by `assertModerationAccessController`. The feature has no dedicated routes — all surfaces are embedded in post, chat, and profile features via the adapter layer.

**Source Path:** apps/VCSM/src/features/moderation/
**Engine Path:** engines/chat/src/controller/markConversationSpam.controller.js, engines/chat/src/dal/moderationActions.write.dal.js — parallel conversation spam path, self-contained in chat engine

---

---

## Active Controllers

| Controller | Purpose | Auth Gate |
|---|---|---|
| assertModerationAccess.controller.js | Guard: verifies actorId holds moderation role before any privileged write | Calls `isModerationAuthorizedDAL` — currently BROKEN (UUID mismatch; always throws FORBIDDEN) |
| report.controller.js | Creates `moderation.reports` rows + `moderation.report_events` audit trail; idempotency via dedupeKey | None — any authenticated actor can file a report |
| moderationActions.controller.js | Admin actions: `hideReportedObjectController` + `dismissReportController` — update reports, insert moderation.actions, hide post/message globally | `assertModerationAccessController` called first — gated but broken |
| postVisibility.controller.js | Actor-scoped post hide/unhide; reads moderation.actions for actor+postId set; "latest action wins" | actorId presence check only — no ownership gate |
| commentVisibility.controller.js | Actor-scoped comment hide/unhide; propagates hidden roots to descendants | actorId presence check only — no ownership gate |
| getConversationCoverStatus.controller.js | Read: is this conversation hidden/covered for this actor? | actorId+conversationId presence check only |
| undoConversationCover.controller.js | Undo conversation spam cover: deletes hide action, restores inbox folder, restores last message pointer | actorId+conversationId presence check only |

---

## Active DALs

| DAL | Tables | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| assertModerationAccess.dal.js | `learning.is_current_user_platform_admin` (RPC) | CRITICAL BUG: vc UUID passed but learning admin RPC resolves via auth.uid() — UUID type mismatch means no moderator action can succeed |
| reports.dal.js | `moderation.reports`, `moderation.report_events`, `moderation.actions`, `vc.posts`, `chat.messages`, `chat.inbox_entries` | Primary write DAL; also contains BRIDGE logic (spam report on conversation → inbox folder upsert) |
| reports.read.dal.js | `moderation.reports` | Read-only: getReportRowById, getReportRowByDedupeKey |
| reports.dal.columns.js | n/a | Column projection constants for moderation DALs — REPORT_COLUMNS, REPORT_EVENT_COLUMNS, MOD_ACTION_COLUMNS, POST_HIDE_COLUMNS, MESSAGE_HIDE_COLUMNS, INBOX_ENTRY_FOLDER_COLUMNS |
| moderationActions.dal.js | `moderation.actions` | insertModerationActionDAL (DAL-level allowlist enforcement), listModerationActionsForActorOnObjectsDAL, dalGetConversationHideAction, dalDeleteConversationHideAction |
| conversationCover.read.dal.js | `chat.messages` | readLatestConversationMessageDAL — used by undoConversationCover |
| conversationCover.write.dal.js | `chat.inbox_entries` | updateConversationInboxFolderDAL, updateConversationInboxLastMessageDAL |

## Active Hooks

| Hook | Calls | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| useReportFlow.js | `createReportController` | UI state machine for the report modal: open/close/submit lifecycle |
| usePostVisibility.js | `postVisibility.controller` (getHiddenPostIdsForActor, hidePostForActor, unhidePostForActor) | Thin view-model wrapper for post visibility reads and writes |
| useHidePostForActor.js | `postVisibility.controller.hidePostForActor` | Single-action hook: hide one post for current actor |
| useCommentVisibility.js | `commentVisibility.controller` (getHiddenCommentIdsForActor, hideCommentForActor, unhideCommentForActor, expandHiddenRootsToDescendants) | View-model for comment visibility + tree-level expansion |
| useConversationCover.js | `getConversationCoverStatus.controller`, `undoConversationCover.controller` | Hydrates cover state on mount; provides undo handler |

**Adapter hooks** (in adapters/hooks/ — public boundary):
- useCommentVisibility.adapter.js
- useConversationCover.adapter.js
- useHidePostForActor.adapter.js
- usePostVisibility.adapter.js
- useReportFlow.adapter.js

## Engine Dependencies

| Engine | Import Path | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| chat engine | engines/chat/src/controller/markConversationSpam.controller.js | Parallel conversation spam path — not called by feature code, used by chat UI directly; owns membership check + inbox folder move + domain event emission |
| chat engine | engines/chat/src/dal/moderationActions.write.dal.js | Chat-engine-owned write DAL for conversation hide actions; consumed by markConversationSpam controller |

Note: The moderation feature source files do NOT import from engines/. The engine dependency runs in the opposite direction — the chat engine has its own parallel spam/cover path that writes to the same `moderation.actions` table. This creates a dual-write pattern on conversation cover (feature path vs engine path).

---

## Cross-Feature Dependencies

| Feature | What is Imported | Direction |
|---|---|---|
| None | — | The moderation feature is imported by other features (post, chat, profiles) via adapter layer. It does not import from other feature namespaces. |

---

## Authorization Pattern

Two-tier authorization pattern:

**Tier 1 — Any authenticated actor (reporter surface):** `report.controller.js` requires only `reporterActorId` presence. No role check. Any logged-in actor can file a report. Controller-level idempotency via optional `dedupeKey`.

**Tier 2 — Privileged moderator surface:** `moderationActions.controller.js` calls `assertModerationAccessController(moderatorActorId)` as step 0 before any read or write. This gates `hideReportedObjectController` and `dismissReportController`. The gate calls `isModerationAuthorizedDAL` which invokes `learning.is_current_user_platform_admin()` RPC server-side.

**CRITICAL BUG:** `assertModerationAccessController` always throws FORBIDDEN in production. `isModerationAuthorizedDAL` passes a vc actor UUID to the RPC but `learning.platform_admins.actor_id` is FK'd to `learning.actors` (different UUID namespace). The vc UUID will never match. No moderator action can succeed through the application layer until both the app-layer fix and DB Batch 1 (fix `can_manage_domain` vc branch) are applied together.

**Actor-scoped visibility controllers** (`postVisibility`, `commentVisibility`, `getConversationCoverStatus`, `undoConversationCover`) enforce only `actorId` presence — no ownership gate. RLS on `moderation.actions` is the enforcement layer for these surfaces.

---

## Module Independence Classification

**MOSTLY INDEPENDENT**

The moderation feature owns its full DAL→Model→Controller→Hook→Adapter stack. It has no imports from other feature namespaces. It does have a parallel implementation overlap with the chat engine's `markConversationSpam` path (dual-write to `moderation.actions`), which creates a loose coupling at the DB table level rather than a code import dependency.

---

## Architecture State

**FLAGGED**

Two CRITICAL production bugs are confirmed and unresolved: (1) `assertModerationAccessController` always throws FORBIDDEN — no privileged moderation action can succeed; (2) report audit trail is broken due to missing INSERT RLS policy on `moderation.report_events`. Six CARNAGE migration batches are written but none confirmed applied, with a dangerous deployment order constraint (Batch 5 FORCE RLS must not precede Batch 1).

---

## Known Structural Risks

1. **CRITICAL: assertModerationAccess UUID mismatch** — `isModerationAuthorizedDAL` queries `learning.platform_admins` using a vc actor UUID. These are different UUID namespaces. No moderator action can succeed. App-layer fix + DB Batch 1 must ship together.
2. **CRITICAL: report_events audit trail broken** — Reporter INSERT RLS policy missing on `moderation.report_events`; session-level flag disables all audit writes (SEC-002).
3. **CRITICAL: DB migration deployment order** — Batch 5 (FORCE RLS) must never apply before Batch 1 (fix `can_manage_domain`). Violating this order locks out service_role with broken policies.
4. **Dual-write on conversation cover** — Feature layer and chat engine both write to `moderation.actions` for conversation cover. No coordination layer. Potential inconsistency if the two paths diverge.
5. **Missing moderation.moderators table** — Planned but never created. Current auth relies solely on `learning.platform_admins` RPC, creating a cross-schema dependency that is the root of the UUID mismatch bug.
6. **No moderator dashboard** — `hideReportedObjectController` and `dismissReportController` are implemented but have no UI surface. Admin-only actions are blocked by both the UI gap and the auth bug.
7. **BRIDGE logic in DAL layer** — `insertReportRow` in `reports.dal.js` contains domain logic (spam report → inbox folder upsert). This is a DAL-layer boundary violation; business logic belongs in the controller.
8. **No test coverage** — Zero test files identified in the feature.
9. **Group chat block enforcement partial** — Block RLS covers direct chats only; group chats are not enforced.

---

---

## Recommended Handoffs

- **CARNAGE** — 6 migration batches are written but unconfirmed. Batch 1 + app-layer fix must deploy together. Batch 5 must not precede Batch 1. Migration verification is the current blocking P0.
- **ELEKTRA** — Precision security scan not yet run. UUID mismatch, dual-write, and missing RLS policies need formal source→sink chain analysis.
- **SENTRY** — Runtime observability not configured. No alerting on moderation FORBIDDEN errors or failed audit writes.
- **SPIDER-MAN** — Zero test coverage. Regression safety net required before any auth or migration changes ship.
- **IRONMAN** — Feature ownership not formally assigned.

---

## Final Module Status

**MOSTLY COMPLETE**

The feature has a complete architecture stack (controllers, DALs, model, hooks, adapters, components). All layers follow the DAL→Model→Controller→Hook convention. The core report submission flow is functional. However, two CRITICAL production bugs block all privileged moderation actions and the audit trail. No test coverage. No moderator dashboard. Six pending migrations with a dangerous deployment order constraint.
